steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The three notes report three different outcomes — positive, neutral, and negative effects. Any valid generalization must account for this full pattern of variation, not cherry-pick one result or claim consistency.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'effects can vary': Directly reflects the three distinct outcomes shown across the studies, accurately synthesizing all the data. ✓
  • Choice B — 'reversed causality': Flips the direction of the relationship; the studies measure effects in one direction only. ✗
  • Choice C — '2017 study found negative effects': Contradicts the actual findings reported in the notes. ✗
  • Choice D — 'consistent positive effects': Claims uniformity when one study found neutral and another found negative effects. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
